title: RSA/VAPID private keys stored plaintext in SQLite
description:
accounts.private_key_pem and vapid_keys.private_key_pem are stored as cleartext PEM in the SQLite database (db.rs lines 47, 370). If the database file is exfiltrated (backup theft, directory traversal, host compromise), the attacker obtains all federation signing keys and the VAPID key — enabling them to impersonate the server to any federated peer and send spoofed push notifications.

Locations: cli.rs line 501 (insert), delivery.rs line 193 (read for signing), push.rs line 61 (read VAPID).

This is standard practice for single-operator ActivityPub servers (Mastodon stores keys the same way), so pragmatic risk is low unless the DB file is exposed. But it is worth noting for any future move to multi-tenant or cloud hosting.

Fix: encrypt at rest using the server.secret_key from config (AES-256-GCM with key derived via HKDF from secret_key + purpose label). Decrypt on read in memory only.
close_reason: Addressed by design doc (docs/design-key-storage.md) — implementation is a separate task
